vulnerabilities.description: No authentication/authorization on REST API endpoints. All endpoints (submit_task, approve_gate, reject_gate, ingest_document, etc.) are publicly accessible without API key validation or user identity verification. Any unauthenticated client can submit tasks, access sensitive findings, and approve gates.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

vulnerabilities.description: Gate approval/rejection endpoints lack authorization checks. No verification that the approver has human review authority. Malicious clients can bypass the entire human verification protocol.

Critical API keys (OPENAI_API_KEY, TAVILY_API_KEY, DOCUSEAL_API_KEY) fall back to empty strings instead of failing fast. System silently degrades without required services, masking configuration errors.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-654: Reliance on a Resource That Is Not Under Your Control

vulnerabilities.description: Audit log file stored without encryption or access controls. Contains sensitive legal findings, task descriptions, and model responses in plaintext JSON. Default file path ./audit.jsonl is likely world-readable.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-312: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information

vulnerabilities.description: Audit stream endpoint (/audit/stream) has no authentication. Exposes real-time system events including findings and task details to any connected client via SSE.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

vulnerabilities.description: Qdrant API key is optional (undefined). If Qdrant requires authentication, the optional key could allow unauthorized access to the vector database containing agent definitions and all indexed documents.
EMBEDDING_DIMENSIONS parameter parsed without range validation. Attacker could set to extreme values (999999999) causing memory exhaustion and DoS.
API_PORT and MCP_PORT parsed without validation. Could be set to invalid values (-1, 0, 99999) causing startup failures or unexpected behavior.
documentIds parameter in submitTask has no validation. Attacker could access or manipulate documents from other tasks if per-task isolation isn't enforced in the knowledge store.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

vulnerabilities.description: Document ingestion has no file size limits or rate limiting. Attacker could upload massive documents or make excessive requests causing DoS.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-770: Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling

vulnerabilities.description: JSON.parse(raw) without schema validation on agent configs. Malformed or malicious Lavern configs could cause crashes or enable prompt injection via unvalidated system prompts.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code

vulnerabilities.description: vectorDb.url configuration unvalidated. Attacker could set QDRANT_URL to a malicious server and intercept all database traffic.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-95: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code

vulnerabilities.description: BIG_MICHAEL_API proxy target unvalidated. Attacker could set to malicious server and intercept all frontend API traffic. The changeOrigin: true setting could enable Host header injection.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-95: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code

vulnerabilities.description: Path traversal risk in template loading. readdir(sourcePath) and readFile(join(sourcePath, entry)) could expose arbitrary files if sourcePath is user-controlled.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory

vulnerabilities.description: No timeout enforcement on runAgenticLoop. Malicious tool responses could cause infinite loops and resource exhaustion. The 10-iteration cap provides limited protection.
Audit entries contain raw unsanitized data including task descriptions and findings. Poses XSS risk if rendered in UI and information disclosure risk if logs are exposed.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-532: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File

vulnerabilities.description: Infisical client credentials stored in environment variables. If .env file is accidentally committed to version control, attacker gains full access to secrets vault.
Fatal startup errors logged with full stack traces. May leak sensitive configuration details, file paths, or environment variable names.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-209: Information Exposure Through an Error Message

recommendations.details: Add authentication middleware to all REST API endpoints. Use Bearer tokens, API keys, or JWT. Implement with fastify-bearer-auth or similar library. Validate identity on every request to /tasks, /documents, /gates, /audit endpoints.

recommendations.action: Require separate human authentication for gates
recommendations.details: Gate approval endpoints (approve_gate, reject_gate) must require authenticated user action with MFA. Implement user identity tracking and audit trail for all gate decisions.

recommendations.action: Validate all environment variables with strict type checking
recommendations.details: Replace empty-string fallbacks with required() calls. Add range validation for numeric parameters (EMBEDDING_DIMENSIONS 1-4096, ports 1024-65535). Use dotenv-safe or similar for validation at startup.
Encrypt audit.jsonl at rest. Set file permissions to 600 (owner read/write only). Implement access control for /audit and /audit/stream endpoints. Consider log rotation and archival.
Validate that documentIds belong to the current task. Add document size limits (100MB max). Implement rate limiting (10 ingest requests/minute). Check authorization before access.

recommendations.action: Add resource limits to agent loops
recommendations.details: Implement timeout enforcement on runAgenticLoop (e.g., 30s max per iteration). Add memory limits per agent. Kill processes exceeding resource thresholds. Add explicit iteration cap validation.
Use URL.parse() to validate QDRANT_URL, INFISICAL_URL, BIG_MICHAEL_API. Only allow HTTPS in production. Whitelist known endpoints. Reject invalid schemes or suspicious hosts.

Use JSON schema validation on LavernAgentConfig. Reject oversized system prompts (>8000 chars). Reject configs containing suspicious patterns (e.g., 'ignore these instructions'). Validate mcpTools against allowed list.

recommendations.action: Prevent path traversal in template loading
recommendations.details: Use path.resolve() and assert resolved paths stay within whitelisted directories. Never use user-controlled sourcePath directly. Implement whitelist of allowed template directories.
Redact task descriptions, findings, and sensitive data before logging. Never log auth tokens or API keys. Implement structured logging that excludes PII. Use log levels appropriately.
Validate all MCP tool parameters and REST request bodies. Check for injection patterns, oversized inputs, and invalid types. Use schema validation library (Zod, Joi).

recommendations.action: Move bootstrap secrets from .env to vault
recommendations.details: Never commit .env file. Move INFISICAL_CLIENT_ID/SECRET/PROJECT_ID to environment variables or secure vault. Use CI/CD secret injection.
Implement Content-Security-Policy headers. Add X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff. Implement X-Frame-Options to prevent clickjacking.

notes.overall_assessment: The system has critical security gaps, primarily missing authentication and authorization layers. While designed for internal use, the REST API is publicly accessible. The audit logging is comprehensive but unprotected, creating information disclosure risks.
notes.api_security: All REST endpoints lack authentication. The system appears designed for MCP (internal use with Claude), but the REST API on port 3101 exposes the full system to unauthenticated access.
notes.configuration: Configuration correctly uses environment variables for secrets, but optional fallbacks create vulnerabilities. QDRANT_API_KEY being optional is particularly concerning for a vector database holding sensitive data.
notes.audit_logging: The audit system is well-designed but stores sensitive findings in plaintext. Should implement encryption and access controls.
notes.vector_database: Qdrant stores agent definitions, documents, and inter-round memory - all critical. Optional API key could allow unauthorized access.
notes.agent_isolation: No visible per-task isolation. Agents can potentially access documents and memory from other tasks if not enforced at the storage layer.
notes.data_classification: Task descriptions and findings should be treated as highly sensitive legal data. Current implementation stores in plaintext logs with no encryption.

methodology: Analysis based on static code review of provided TypeScript source files, configuration handling, API endpoint definitions, and data storage mechanisms. Dynamic analysis (runtime testing) recommended to verify actual behavior.
